mlas.name: Naomi Long
mlas.bio_short: Leader of the Alliance Party and Justice Minister since 2020. A former Belfast Lord Mayor and Westminster MP, Long has championed cross-community reconciliation and restorative justice. She introduced the Integrated Education Act as a private member's bill — the first significant education reform in a generation.

pledge_delivery_score.source: MLA Tracker — voting record and pledge analysis
pledge_delivery_score.method: Weighted pledge delivery: backed pledges score 100, partial 50, mismatch 0. MLA-specific pledges weighted 60%, party platform 40%.

party_line_voting_pct.method: Division votes where MLA's recorded vote matches party whip position, Jan 2022–May 2026. Excludes free votes and abstentions on conscience matters.

chamber_contributions_total.method: Count of unique contributions (debates, questions, statements) attributed to this MLA in Official Report, Jan 2022–May 2026.

committee_attendance_pct.method: Meetings attended as proportion of meetings scheduled, all committees on which MLA sits. Approved absences excluded.

evidence.value: Integrated Education Act (NI) 2022 enacted.

evidence.value: Restorative Justice Regulation 2023 enacted; 14 community restorative justice organisations now regulated.
Academic selection retained. No legislation introduced.

evidence.value: Mental Health Strategy 2021-31 adopted with £80m ring-fenced in 2022-23; subsequent years underfunded by 22%.
evidence.method: Budget line comparison with strategy targets.

contributions.excerpt: The justice system's approach to serious sexual offences must centre the survivor's experience from initial report through to sentencing.
Will the Minister confirm the timetable for the implementation regulations under the Integrated Education Act?
The Department has published its response to the Gillen Review recommendations on serious sexual offences trials.
This bill closes a gap that has left hate crime victims without adequate protection for too long.
Regulation of community restorative justice organisations is now complete, with 14 organisations on the statutory register.
The Department will bring forward amendments to the Criminal Justice (Aggravated Offences) Bill at consideration stage.

mlas.name: Gavin Robinson
mlas.bio_short: Gavin Robinson served as DUP leader 2021-2024, steering the party through the Stormont boycott over the Northern Ireland Protocol. A barrister and former Lord Mayor of Belfast, Robinson has represented Belfast East at both Stormont and Westminster. He resigned the DUP leadership in April 2024 and continues as an MLA.

pledge_delivery_score.source: MLA Tracker — voting record and pledge analysis
pledge_delivery_score.method: Weighted pledge delivery. Score reduced significantly by 22-month Stormont boycott during which no Executive function operated.

party_line_voting_pct.method: Division votes where MLA's recorded vote matches party whip position, Jan 2022–May 2026. Excludes free votes.

chamber_contributions_total.method: Count of unique contributions attributed to this MLA in Official Report. Lower total reflects boycott period absence.

committee_attendance_pct.method: Meetings attended as proportion of meetings scheduled. Low score reflects boycott period where committees were suspended.

pledges.title: Remove Irish Sea border checks through Protocol

evidence.value: Windsor Framework 2023 reduced checks on retail goods; core agri-food checks remain.
evidence.method: Policy outcome comparison with manifesto text.

pledges.title: Protect grammar schools and academic selection

evidence.value: Grammar schools fully operational. No abolition legislation passed.
Identity and Language Act 2022 enacted at Westminster despite DUP opposition.

pledges.title: Deliver a health recovery plan with reduced waiting lists

evidence.value: Stormont boycott meant no Executive health budget 2022-24. Waiting lists reached record highs.
evidence.method: Waiting list data comparison with mandate start.

pledges.title: Protect small business rates through targeted relief

evidence.value: Small Business Rate Relief extended annually 2022-2026.

contributions.excerpt: The Windsor Framework represents progress, but the democratic deficit at its heart remains unaddressed.
Will the Finance Minister confirm that the small business rate relief scheme will be extended into 2026-27?
The waiting list crisis was made worse by the institutional collapse this party helped engineer.
Grammar schools represent the gold standard in NI education. We will not allow ideological abolition.

mlas.name: Michelle O'Neill
mlas.bio_short: Michelle O'Neill became Northern Ireland's first nationalist First Minister in February 2024, following the DUP's return to power-sharing. A former Agriculture Minister, she has led Sinn Féin in Stormont since 2017. A key figure in the New Decade, New Approach agreement, O'Neill has championed Irish language rights, healthcare reform, and cross-border cooperation throughout her career.

mlas.name: Doug Beattie
mlas.bio_short: Doug Beattie has led the Ulster Unionist Party since 2021, seeking to modernise Ulster unionism on a socially liberal platform. A decorated veteran of the British Army with service in Kosovo and Afghanistan, Beattie has championed veterans' welfare, mental health reform, and environmental legislation. He has publicly broken with traditional UUP positions on some equality matters.

mlas.name: Matthew O'Toole
mlas.bio_short: Matthew O'Toole leads the SDLP from the opposition benches at Stormont. A former adviser in the UK Cabinet Office and Treasury, O'Toole brings a Westminster policy background to Northern Irish politics. First elected in 2022, he has been a vocal critic of the DUP's Stormont boycott and has championed economic reform, housing affordability, and European engagement.
